title: Definition of Information
definition: Information means any material in any form including records, documents, memos, e-mails, opinions, advices, press releases, circulars, orders, logbooks, contracts, reports, papers, samples, models, data material held in any electronic form.
practical_implication: RTI only covers EXISTING information. PIO is not required to create, compile, or provide opinions. If the information does not exist in record form, the RTI cannot be fulfilled.
chips_relevance: CHiPS must only provide information that exists in its records. Requests for analysis, reports, or opinions not already documented can be declined.
common_mistakes: PIOs sometimes try to answer opinion-seeking RTIs. This is not required under the Act.
source_reference: RTI Act 2005, Section 2(f); CG RTI Rules 2005

title: Right to Information
definition: The right to inspect works, documents, records, take notes, extracts, certified copies and obtain information in electronic formats.
practical_implication: Applicants may seek copies, inspections and electronic records.
chips_relevance: CHiPS may provide files, reports, logs, contracts, database extracts and certified copies where permissible.
common_mistakes: Limiting disclosure only to physical documents.

title: Proactive Disclosure
definition: Every public authority shall publish key organizational, functional and decision-making information proactively.
practical_implication: Information already covered under proactive disclosure should be publicly available without requiring RTI filing.
chips_relevance: Department information, schemes, tenders, budgets, officers and functions should be proactively disclosed.
common_mistakes: Using RTI responses instead of maintaining proactive disclosure.

title: Appointment of Public Information Officer
definition: Every public authority shall designate Public Information Officers to provide information under the Act.
practical_implication: PIO is the statutory authority responsible for RTI processing.
chips_relevance: All recommendations generated by the AI remain advisory and require PIO review.
common_mistakes: Delegating legal responsibility to support staff or systems.

title: Seeking Assistance
definition: PIO may seek assistance from any officer for proper discharge of duties.
practical_implication: Information may be collected from multiple departments before responding.
chips_relevance: Useful when information resides with project teams, NIC, SWAN, Data Center, or other CHiPS units.
common_mistakes: Rejecting applications because information is held by another internal unit.

title: Deemed Public Information Officer
definition: An officer whose assistance is sought shall be treated as a deemed PIO.
practical_implication: Such officers share accountability under the Act.
chips_relevance: Project managers and custodians of records may become deemed PIOs.
common_mistakes: Assuming only the designated PIO bears responsibility.

title: Making an RTI Application
definition: Citizens may request information through a written or electronic application.
practical_implication: Applications must clearly identify the information sought.
chips_relevance: Online RTI portal requests fall under this section.
common_mistakes: Rejecting applications because they are submitted electronically.

title: Transfer of RTI Application
definition: Where an application is made to a public authority for information which is held by another public authority, the PIO shall transfer the application or part thereof to the other public authority within 5 days and inform the applicant.
practical_implication: Transfer must happen within 5 days. The applicant must be informed. The transferred authority then has 30 days to respond (not 30 minus the days already spent).
chips_relevance: Most non-IT RTIs received by CHiPS should be transferred under Section 6(3)(2) to the relevant department.
common_mistakes: Transferring without informing applicant. Counting transfer days as part of the 30-day window incorrectly.

title: Time Limit for Disposal
definition: Information shall ordinarily be provided within 30 days.
practical_implication: The statutory response deadline is 30 days.
chips_relevance: Can be displayed in workflow dashboards and reminders.

title: Rejection Communication Requirements
definition: Rejection must specify reasons, appeal period and appellate authority.
practical_implication: Every denial must contain appeal guidance.
chips_relevance: Auto-generated rejection letters should always include appeal information.

title: National Security Exemption
definition: Information affecting sovereignty, security or strategic interests may be exempt.
practical_implication: Sensitive security information may be withheld.
chips_relevance: Critical infrastructure or cyber-security information may trigger review.

title: Commercial Confidence
definition: Commercial confidence, trade secrets and intellectual property may be exempt.
chips_relevance: Software contracts, bid evaluations and proprietary architecture documents may be affected.

title: Fiduciary Relationship
definition: Information available in fiduciary capacity may be exempt.
practical_implication: Information held in trust relationships requires scrutiny.
chips_relevance: Certain citizen data repositories may invoke fiduciary considerations.

title: Personal Information Exemption
definition: Information which relates to personal information, the disclosure of which has no relationship to any public activity or interest, or which would cause unwarranted invasion of the privacy of the individual.
practical_implication: This exemption has TWO conditions: (1) no public interest relationship AND (2) would invade privacy. Both must be true. Public officials acting in official capacity are NOT covered by this exemption.
chips_relevance: Employee personal details (salary, home address, family) are covered. But employee official conduct, project assignments, and official decisions are NOT covered.
common_mistakes: Using 8(1)(j) to protect all employee information including official acts.
source_reference: RTI Act 2005, Section 8(1)(j); CIC Order No. CIC/SA/A/2012/000011

title: Severability
definition: Exempt portions may be redacted while the remaining information is disclosed.
practical_implication: RTIs should not be rejected entirely when only a portion is exempt.
chips_relevance: Enable partial disclosure workflows with automatic redaction recommendations.
common_mistakes: Rejecting entire records due to limited exempt content.

title: Third Party Information Procedure
definition: Where information relates to or has been supplied by a third party and the third party has treated it as confidential, the PIO shall give notice to the third party and allow them to make representation within 10 days before deciding on disclosure.
practical_implication: MANDATORY procedure — cannot be skipped. Third party gets 10 days to respond. PIO then has 40 days total (not 30) to give final decision. Failure to follow Section 11 = procedural violation.
chips_relevance: Any RTI about vendor contracts, third-party software, or data provided by companies to CHiPS in confidence must go through Section 11 procedure.
common_mistakes: Disclosing third-party commercial data without Section 11 notice. This is a serious violation.

title: Appeals
definition: Provides for first and second appeal mechanisms.
chips_relevance: Appeal information should appear in rejection and partial-disclosure responses.

title: 150-Word Application Limit
definition: Under Chhattisgarh RTI Rules, an RTI request shall ordinarily relate to one subject matter and should not exceed 150 words. Applications exceeding this may be returned for resubmission.
practical_implication: If the application exceeds 150 words or asks about multiple unrelated subjects, PIO may return it and ask for resubmission as separate applications.
chips_relevance: CHiPS PIO should check word count and subject unity before processing. Multi-subject RTIs should be bifurcated into separate applications.
common_mistakes: Rejecting applications outright based on word limit instead of asking for resubmission.
